I don't think so because there are very similar genexps near listcomps, for example:

```python
N2 = math.prod(bs[axis] for axis in axes_b)               # <- genexp
newshape_a = (math.prod([as_[ax] for ax in notin]), N2)   # <- listcomp
```

I suspect they're ancient artifacts or just written as-is (no implications behind) and suggest coherently replacing them with modern genexps.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/2511050746/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

Done.

> turn on a lint for this

That's the point of this PR: always using genexp is maintainer-friendly and may be preferable in general.
I came to understand listcomp is slightly faster than genexp (thank you @seberg , I didn't know that) but for long iterators genexp is still preferred for memory optimization, and I personally believe that merits of the latter surpluses the former, especially when the project is so big (`numpy` is big project, isn't it?) that maintainers cannot examine every single line of the codes and perform microbench for each iterator reduction.
By the way if you maintainers are interested in `ruff` integration, I'm willing to work on that.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/2515346047/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

### Problem

Floating-point number can satisfy **BOTH** `a != b` and `b - a == 0`.

```py
import cupy as cp

# 1.1754944e-38
a = cp.finfo(cp.float32).smallest_normal
# 1.1754945e-38
b = cp.nextafter(a, 1)

assert a != b
assert b - a == 0
```

This PR resolves possible division-by-zero error by computing `xb - xa` in should be the necessary changes for Cython-3:

1. Resolve some compilation errors
2. Enable a backward compatible mode of Cython: `special binary operator` set to True.

I applied these 2 patches on top of https://github.com/cupy/cupy/pull/8319, and built cupy-13.2.0 on ROCm 6. My cupy application run without errors.

I also observed one deprecation warning regarding Cython, which is currently not a blocker but may become one in the future:

> The 'IF' statement is deprecated and will be removed in a future Cython version. Consider using runtime conditions or C macros instead. is a rebase/redo of https://github.com/cupy/cupy/pull/8314, which picked way too many conflicts.

Running tests locally under NumPy 2.1.0 gives several failures. Before fixing them, I'd like to clarify the vision:
- CuPy 13.2 aims to support both Numpy 1.26 and 2.x
- in this PR, do we need tests to pass under NumPy 1.x?
- do we keep shims for things removed in NumPy 2.0?
- do we emit DeprecationWarnings for things deprecated in NumPy 2.0?
- do we track NumPy 2.0.0 or can jump straight to 2.1.0? Currently integer arrays cannot be compared with big integers.

```python
import numpy as np

np.asarray([1, 2, 3], dtype=np.int64) < 10**1024 # [True, True, True]
np.asarray([1, 2, 3], dtype=np.float64) < 10**1024 # OverflowError

import cupy as cp

cp.asarray([1, 2, 3], dtype=cp.int64) < 10**1024 # OverflowError
cp.asarray([1, 2, 3], dtype=cp.float64) < 10**1024 # OverflowError
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/8709/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/8709/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

- create `.ruff.toml` mostly compatible with the current linting configuration

- fix invalid `noqa` directives

{"action":"opened","number":8629,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/pulls/8629","id":2095141182,"node_id":"PR_kwDOBFKgkM584VE-","html_url":"https://github.com/cupy/cupy/pull/8629","diff_url":"https://github.com/cupy/cupy/pull/8629.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/cupy/cupy/pull/8629.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/8629","number":8629,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"Make `cupy.linalg.solve` compatible with `numpy` v2","user":{"login":"EarlMilktea","id":66886825,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjY2ODg2ODI1","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/66886825?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/EarlMilktea","html_url":"https://github.com/EarlMilktea","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/EarlMilktea/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/EarlMilktea/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/EarlMilktea/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/EarlMilktea/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/EarlMilktea/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/EarlMilktea/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/EarlMilktea/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/EarlMilktea/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/EarlMilktea/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Related to #8306 .


### Description of changes

Now shape of `b` (left hand side) must be either `(M,)` or `(..., M, K)` rather than `(..., M)` or `(..., M, K)` (`numpy` v1).

See https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.linalg.solve.html#numpy.linalg.solve for Use [Ruff](https://github.com/astral-sh/ruff) instead of `flake8` and `autopep8`, and also introduce `isort`-equivalent formatting.

## Description of Changes

- `flake8`: Replaced by `ruff check`. It is designed to be a drop-in replacement of `flake8` without any config.
- `autopep8`: Replaced by `ruff format`.
- Ruff rule `I`: Sorts import as PEP8 specifies (a.k.a. `isort`)

### Additional Information

## Pros

- Reduce diffs. by aggressive formatting
- Reduce dependency
- Better performance
- Can introduce more linting rules in the future
- Can format example codes in docstrings
- Support `.ipynb`

## Cons

- Still under active development: may need to pin version
- Introduce massive changes on first formatting","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/8481/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/8481/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}
